title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?
summary: Many of the more than 100 people awaiting processing on the tiny Pacific island are skipping meals to make ends meet, a new survey has foundFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastMost refugees and asylum seekers held in Nauru’s detention centres say they cannot afford to buy enough food or access clean drinking water, despite the Australian government spending the equivalent of more than $9m per person last financial year.

title: The First Chatbot’s Multiple Personalities
summary: ELIZA is remembered as the world’s first AI star, a kindly therapist in chatbot form that gently probed users’ worries. Even its creator, Joseph Weizenbaum, was surprised by the warm reception given to his experiment in human-machine interaction. For some, it heralded an age of automated psychotherapy, while others believed the program demonstrated sentience, a fallacy soon known as the “ELIZA effect.” Based on published descriptions, ELIZA has been implemented on many different computers, but o
title_cn: 第一个聊天机器人的多重性格
summary_cn: ELIZA 被认为是世界上第一位人工智能明星，是一位以聊天机器人形式出现的友善治疗师，可以温和地探究用户的担忧。就连它的创造者 Joseph Weizenbaum 也对它受到的热烈欢迎感到惊讶。

【标题】第一个聊天机器人的多重性格

title: We’re Squandering LEDs’ Potential to Save Our Night Skies
summary: In the chill of a London spring night, under overcast skies, iconic Trafalgar Square opens around me. Admiral Nelson rises on his pedestal, the National Gallery rests behind, the church of St Martin-in-the-Fields sits nearby. From the 13th century, the site served as the Royal Mews for hawks and then horses.
